data.title: Riverview Bancorp reports Q4 FY2026 loss after $11.4M securities sale; adjusted EPS $0.03
data.description:
Riverview Bancorp reported a net loss of $8.0 million, or $(0.39) per diluted share, for the fourth fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2026, which included an $11.4 million pre-tax loss on the sale of lower-yielding securities tied to a strategic balance sheet optimization. Excluding the securities restructuring, the company reported net income of $656,000, or $0.03 per diluted share (non-GAAP). For fiscal 2026, Riverview posted a net loss of $4.3 million inclusive of the restructuring, and net income excluding the optimization of $4.4 million, or $0.21 per diluted share (non-GAAP).

Financial Highlights

  • Net interest income: $10.18 million for Q4 FY2026 (compared with $9.19 million in Q4 FY2025).
  • Net interest margin (NIM): 2.92% in Q4 FY2026, up from 2.65% in Q4 FY2025; full-year NIM 2.86% for FY2026.
  • Non-interest income (GAAP): loss of $8.03 million in Q4 FY2026 reflecting an $11.35 million loss on sale of securities; non-GAAP non-interest income excluding the optimization: $3.32 million for the quarter.
  • Net income (GAAP): $(8.04) million for Q4 FY2026; Net income excluding securities restructure (non-GAAP): $656,000 for the quarter. Basic and diluted EPS (non-GAAP): $0.03 for Q4.
  • Allowance for credit losses: $15.25 million at March 31, 2026; net loan charge-offs of $1.10 million in the quarter; provision for credit losses for the quarter: $1.16 million.

Business Highlights

  • Strategic balance sheet optimization implemented March 25, 2026: reclassified HTM securities to AFS and sold $149.3 million of lower-yielding securities (avg yield 1.62%), generating $137.9 million cash proceeds to redeploy into higher-yielding assets and loan growth.
  • Deployment activity: purchased $24.7 million of bonds late in the quarter with a weighted average yield of 4.95%; post-optimization portfolio duration approximately 6.0 years and estimated 12-month investment cash flows of ~$16.7 million.
  • Loan growth and pipeline: total loans $1.08 billion at March 31, 2026, up $7.4 million for the quarter; new originations of $46.3 million in the quarter and a loan pipeline of $56.4 million.
  • Deposit and liquidity management: total deposits $1.25 billion at March 31, 2026 with shifts toward non-interest and money market accounts; available liquidity of approximately $593.7 million including FHLB and Federal Reserve lines.
  • Capital and shareholder actions: tangible book value per share (non-GAAP) $5.76 at March 31, 2026; Board adopted $4.0 million stock repurchase plan and repurchased 130,059 shares in the quarter; quarterly cash dividend of $0.02 per share paid April 24, 2026.

Original SEC Filing: RIVERVIEW BANCORP INC [ RVSB ] - 8-K - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Jana Partners Calls on Markel Group to Divest From Ventures Arm, Repurchase $2B in Stock
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Hedge fund Jana Partners is calling on Markel Group to divest from Markel Ventures and repurchase $2 billion worth of shares through a tender offer.

The fund sent a letter to Markel's board on Thursday, arguing that the company's model of combining insurance operations with an investment arm has led it to underperform its insurance peers.

"Markel's ongoing poor performance for shareholders can no longer be attributed to an underperforming insurance segment nor the market failing to understand the company's 'unique flywheel' of combining insurance and ventures," the fund wrote.

"The market understands it perfectly and has instead decided, today and nearly every day over the past decade, that the current structure produces sub-peer shareholder returns, creates no unique value and warrants a discounted multiple," Jana added.

Markel's strategy of buying back undervalued shares on the open market is limited by its low liquidity, and doesn't address the companies' structural discount in the market, the fund wrote. It called for a $2 billion tender offer for shares in parallel with a divestiture of the investment arm.

"Rather than continuing to pursue a strategy that is not working, it is time for the Board to prioritize shareholder returns and unlock Markel's value through a large-scale tender and subsequent divestiture of the ventures business," the fund wrote.

Markel did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Microsoft Seen Moving Decisively To Usage-Based Pricing Model — Market Talk
data.description: Microsoft looks to be shifting decisively toward usage-based pricing for its artificial-intelligence products, UBS analysts say in a note. "Microsoft and its software peers have been marching towards a usage-based pricing model for well over a year now, but this earnings call might be remembered as the moment when this pace suddenly shifted," they say. Though it's clear that AI coding agents are driving token consumption, they say, the picture is less clear for non-coding use cases, and the timeline for the pricing shift becoming accretive is uncertain. "The key questions are whether the AI features being charged for on a consumption basis are truly value-add enough to drive usage and whether large enterprise will be willing to absorb higher token costs without cutting even further into their non-AI software budgets." (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Silver Bow Mining, Silver and Base Metals Exploration Company, Files for NYSE American IPO
data.description:
Silver Bow Mining filed a 424B4 prospectus for its initial public offering on the NYSE American under the ticker SBMT. The company is offering 5,200,000 common shares at $11.50 per share for gross proceeds of $59.8 million. Shares began trading on April 30, 2026.

Business Description

Silver Bow Mining is a minerals exploration company focused on silver, zinc, gold, lead, and copper targets in Montana. The company controls approximately 4,193 acres of patented mineral claims in Silver Bow County, anchored by its material property, the 878‑acre Rainbow Block in the historic Butte Mining District.

The Rainbow Block benefits from extensive historical exploration, mining and metallurgical work. An S‑K 1300 Technical Report established an initial Inferred Mineral Resource of 11.48 million tons grading 14.8 oz/ton silver equivalent (Ag‑Eq), containing 170.0 million ounces Ag‑Eq, with no Measured or Indicated resources or Mineral Reserves. Near‑term plans include surface and underground drilling, underground rehabilitation and development to advance resource confidence and support future engineering studies. Additional blocks (Marget Ann, Goldsmith, Great Republic, Travona, and Emma) provide district‑scale exploration potential.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: Exposure to silver, zinc, gold, lead and copper demand across industrial and precious metals markets
  • Market growth: Silver demand supported by electronics and solar; base metals tied to infrastructure and industrial cycles
  • Market position: Early-stage explorer with a material property (Rainbow Block) in the historic Butte Mining District
  • Key competitors: Other North American silver and base metal explorers and producers operating in U.S. and Canada
  • Industry trends: Electrification, renewable energy, and infrastructure spending underpin medium-term demand for silver and zinc

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: Pre-revenue explorer; no commercial customers
  • Locations: Headquarters in Butte, Montana; properties in Silver Bow County, Montana
  • Geographic presence: United States (Montana)
  • Partnerships: Underwriting led by Cantor Fitzgerald & Co.; various technical consultants including Dahrouge Geological Consulting
  • Orders/GMV: Not applicable (exploration-stage)
  • Other key metrics: ~4,193 acres mineral rights; ~1,410 acres surface rights; Rainbow Block Inferred Mineral Resource of 11.48M tons @ 14.8 oz/ton Ag‑Eq (170.0M oz Ag‑Eq); planned 35,000 ft surface drilling and underground drilling; workforce of 12 employees and 6 contractors as of April 29, 2026

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): No operating revenue (exploration-stage)
  • Revenue growth: Not applicable
  • Operating income: Operating loss of $10,330,074 (2025)
  • Net income: Net loss of $10,373,939 (2025)

Management

  • C. Travis Naugle, Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer - Mining executive and licensed Professional Engineer with 25+ years developing copper, zinc, silver and gold projects; former CEO of Lion Copper and Gold.
  • Wade Black, Chief Financial Officer - 31-year Wall Street veteran; co-founded and led broker-dealers; prior board experience in mining; BA and MBA from Columbia University.
  • Kevin Shiell, Chief Operating Officer - Underground mine operations leader with senior roles at I-80 Gold, Americas Gold and Silver, Hecla Mining, and Stillwater Mining.
  • Phillip Nickerson, Vice President of Exploration - Economic geologist; former Principal/Exploration Manager at Rio Tinto; Ph.D. in Geoscience (University of Arizona).

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: SILVER BOW MINING CORP.
  • Filing date: April 30, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: SBMT
  • Exchange: NYSE American
  • Price range: $11.50 per share
  • Offering size: $59,800,000
  • Shares offered: 5,200,000 common shares (plus 780,000 overallotment option)
  • Lead underwriters: Cantor Fitzgerald & Co. (representative); Research Capital USA Inc.; Joseph Gunnar & Co., LLC; Roth Capital Partners, LLC; R.F. Lafferty & Co., Inc.
  • Use of proceeds: Surface exploration drilling; underground rehabilitation and exploration tunnels; underground drilling; metallurgical test work; environmental baseline studies; NYSE American listing fees; salaries; professional fees; and general corporate and working capital.

Original SEC Filing: SILVER BOW MINING CORP. - 424B4 - Apr. 30, 2026

data.title: Union Pacific, Norfolk Southern Refile Rail-Merger Application — WSJ
data.description:
By Esther Fung

The proposed $71.5 billion merger between Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern to form a coast-to-coast railroad run by a single operator will have a projected 39% of the market share of rail freight in the U.S., the two railroads said.

The companies on Thursday resubmitted their merger application to U.S. regulators, this time including a description of conditions that would compel Union Pacific to walk away from the merger. Such conditions typically include requirements that the buyers cede control of certain parts of the railroad and concessions that allow other rail carriers access to its rail lines and facilities.

In January, the Surface Transportation Board rejected the railroads' initial application, saying that it was incomplete, and invited the railroads to revise their application. Market-share projections and a list of conditions that would cause Union Pacific to walk away were among the items that the regulator had asked for when it asked the two companies to re-apply. Some of the information is confidential and redacted in the public filing.

The Surface Transportation Board is overseeing the lengthy merger-review process and will decide to approve or reject the merger. If the board accepts the latest application, it will review public comment and rebuttals in the coming months before making a decision sometime in 2027.

The proposed deal already has drawn skepticism from several lawmakers, state attorneys general and rail customers. They have asked the regulator to scrutinize the transaction, asserting that the combined company could concentrate market power, stifle competition and lead to higher prices and poorer service. Other railroads have lined up against it, saying that the rail industry is consolidated enough.

The two railroads, which control tracks on opposite sides of the Mississippi River, said that the merger merits include reducing bottlenecks at the current interchange terminals which would speed up long-haul deliveries to retailers and factories. The revised application provided a new estimate that the merger will lead to $3.5 billion in savings to shippers, based on a projection that shippers won't have to use as many trucks. The applicants said the merged railroad will take 2.1 million trucks off the roads.

"This merger enhances competition and delivers real public benefits," said Jim Vena, chief executive officer of Union Pacific.

The applicants added that they will add a new service connecting Northern California to the Southeast, and that the overall expected increase in business will drive 1,200 new union jobs over three years.

After a series of railroad mergers over the past several decades, a duopoly effectively operates east of the Mississippi in Norfolk Southern and CSX, and a duopoly exists west of the Mississippi in BNSF Railway and Union Pacific. Railroad analysts and investors have said that if Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern were allowed to merge, BNSF could be compelled to merge with CSX as well.

"This did not begin with a customer asking for a UP-NS merger to happen, " said the president and chief executive officer of BNSF, Katie Farmer. "It's driven by Wall Street on the promise of a big shareholder payout. It will eliminate competition, raise costs for consumers, and destabilize the supply chain that powers the American economy."

The attorneys general of Montana, Tennessee, Kansas, North Dakota, South Dakota, Mississippi and Iowa have voiced opposition to the merger, adding that the deal would "shackle domestic manufacturers, energy producers, and farmers with higher rates and fewer shipping options, thereby blunting their competitive edge against foreign rivals, squeezing household budgets, and weakening the supply chains that underpin our national security."

Write to Esther Fung at esther.fung@wsj.com

data.title: AI Buildout Hurts Meta Shares, Helps Caterpillar — WSJ
data.description:
Caitlin McCabe

America's AI buildout is helping stocks Thursday, but not in all the usual places.

Caterpillar shares jumped 10%, pushing the Dow industrials about 1.5% higher in afternoon trading. The construction-equipment company raised its full-year outlook after reporting results juiced by strong demand from AI-supporting data centers.

The Nasdaq composite meanwhile up about 0.5% after quarterly results from some of America's biggest tech companies. Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta Platforms and Amazon all reported bumper earnings yesterday, partly thanks to the proliferation of artificial-intelligence tools.

Yet not all investors were satisfied, with the tech stocks diverging. Meta shares fell 9% after the Facebook owner said it would spend more money on AI data centers. Microsoft slumped 5% on its own cost concerns. Alphabet rose 9% after the Google parent reported a boom in its cloud business.

The S&P 500 rose about 0.8%, but its technology sector was down on the day, weighed down by a 4% drop in Nvidia. Investors will get another readout from Big Tech when Apple reports earnings after today's close.

Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge showed that higher energy prices from the war in Iran ratcheted up pricing pressures in March. First-quarter GDP also came in weaker than economists expected, as consumer spending slowed.

In energy markets, oil prices jumped overnight before giving up gains. The initial move higher came after Axios reported that President Trump is set to be briefed on new military options for Iran aimed at breaking the deadlock in peace talks. U.S. oil futures slipped about 1.7% to around $105 a barrel.

In Europe, both the European Central Bank and the Bank of England joined the Fed in holding borrowing costs steady.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Gold Regains Ground Amid Sharp Pullback By Crude Oil
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - After moving notably lower over the past few sessions, the price of gold has shown a strong move back to the upside during trading on Thursday.

Gold for June delivery has jumped $68 or 1.5 percent to $4,629.50 an ounce after plunging $179.41 or 3.8 percent over the first three days of the week.

With the sharp increase on the day, the price of gold has bounced well off the nearly one-month closing low set on Wednesday.

The rebound by the price of gold comes amid a significant pullback by the price of crude oil, with Brent crude oil futures tumbling by more than 2.6 percent after skyrocketing over the past several sessions.

Brent crude oil futures surged to a four-year high of $126 a barrel before giving back ground, with the pullback likely reflecting profit taking as it comes amid ongoing concerns about the Middle East conflict.

The U.S. and Iran remain at a stalemate in negotiations over ending the war, with President Donald Trump suggesting the blockade of Iranian ports will remain in place until the countries reach a nuclear deal.

Gold has also benefitted from weakness in the value of the U.S. dollar, with the U.S. dollar index slumping by 0.8 percent.

data.description:
Trinseo PLC reported first-quarter 2026 results with net sales of $724.7M, a year-over-year decline, and a net loss of ($115.9M) that produced diluted loss per share of ($3.2) for the quarter.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$724.7M$784.8M(7.7%)
Net income²($115.9M)($79M)(46.7%)
Diluted EPS³($3.2)($2.22)(44.1%)

¹ Reported as “Net sales”. ² Reported as “Net loss”. ³ Reported as “loss per share‒diluted”.

Business Highlights

  • Demand & sales trend: Net sales fell about 8% YoY due to weaker end‑market demand, lower pricing and reduced volumes across segments.
  • Segment performance: Engineered Materials delivered margin improvement and a roughly 31% increase in adjusted EBITDA, while Polymer Solutions and Latex Binders experienced margin pressure.
  • Cost and operations: Restructuring actions taken in 2025 lowered fixed costs and helped support EBITDA amid difficult market conditions.
  • Market dynamics: Geopolitical volatility and feedstock disruptions limited near‑term recovery but created selective opportunities for volume capture.
  • Liquidity and working capital: Working capital stress and contraction in trade credit materially affected operations and cash conversion in Q1.

data.title: Southern Copper Corp 1Q 2026: Revenue $4.25B, EPS $1.92— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Southern Copper Corp reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue rising to $4.25B and diluted EPS of $1.92, driven by higher metal prices and increased by‑product sales that lifted profitability versus the year-ago quarter.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$4.25B$3.12B36.2%
Net income²$1.58B$945.9M66.7%
Diluted EPS³$1.92$1.1567%

¹ Reported as “Net sales (including sales to related parties, see note 5)”. ² Reported as “Net income attributable to SCC”. ³ Reported as “Per common share amounts attributable to SCC”.

Business Highlights

  • Production: copper output declined 4.0% to 508.3M lbs, while silver rose 11.1% and zinc increased 2.0% year over year.

  • Company maintained ESG momentum with recognition in the S&P Sustainability Yearbook and from Sustainalytics, and received community and water conservation awards.

data.title: CDC Warns Of Drug-Resistant Salmonella Linked To Backyard Birds
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Dozens of people have been sickened and 13 hospitalized by the same strain of Salmonella infection linked to backyard poultry, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

The CDC first reported the outbreak late last week. So far, more than 30 cases have been identified across multiple states, with several hospitalizations. In some instances, the bacteria have also shown resistance to multiple antibiotics.

As of April 23, a total of 34 illnesses had been reported in 13 states. Among the 27 cases with detailed information, no deaths have been reported. Health officials note that the true number of infections is likely higher, as many people with mild symptoms do not seek medical care.

Laboratory testing shows that all patient samples are resistant to fosfomycin, a drug often used when other treatments fail. In eight cases, the bacteria also showed resistance to additional antibiotics, including chloramphenicol, streptomycin, sulfisoxazole, and tetracycline.

The outbreak has been linked to backyard poultry such as chickens and ducks. Even when the birds appear healthy and clean, they can still carry salmonella. People may become infected by handling the animals or anything they come into contact with, then touching their mouth or eating without washing their hands.

The CDC is urging backyard poultry owners to take precautionary measures, such as washing hands thoroughly with soap and water after handling birds, eggs, or related equipment; keeping poultry outdoors; and using dedicated footwear when caring for them. The agency also advises against close contact, such as kissing or snuggling birds near the face.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

data.title: Is Qualcomm Ready for Primetime? What the Technicals Say. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Doug Busch

In a market increasingly defined by connectivity, compute, and edge intelligence, Qualcomm is reasserting itself as a central enabler of the next wave of growth.

Long viewed primarily through the lens of smartphones, the company is now demonstrating broader earnings power across automotive, the internet of things, and AI-driven applications. With multiple end markets stabilizing and new revenue streams gaining traction, the narrative is shifting from cyclical recovery to structural expansion, positioning Qualcomm as a more durable and diversified growth story than the market has historically priced in.

To be frank, Qualcomm has lagged behind an otherwise strong semiconductor group, but the current setup suggests that it may join the broader rally. Of course, the iShares Semiconductor exchange-traded fund recently recorded an 18-session winning streak, its longest run since the fund's inception in 2021. Qualcomm came into Thursday's session down 8% year to date, but will now have some wind at its back. Peers Nvidia and NXPI Semiconductors have made nice moves lately: Nvidia broke above the $200 level in a double-bottom base, and NXPI jumped more than 25% — its best day since 2010 — following a jubilant earnings release.

The stock's daily chart shows a bullish RSI divergence that formed with a higher RSI low as price made a lower low in February and April. That suggested downside momentum was waning even as the stock trended lower.

True to form, the stock rebounded nicely and is now seeking its first four-week winning streak in almost two years. A bullish island reversal was completed with the 11% gap up on April 24 (following an 8.5% gap down on Feb. 5 after its seventh consecutive negative earnings reaction). In early trading, Qualcomm is up 12% after a well-received report, attempting to put that streak behind it. It is approaching a double-bottom pivot of $184.55 as it pushes above the 200-day simple moving average resistance this week. The 50-day simple moving average has also begun to slope higher for the first time since late last year. The stock could reach $265 by early 2027, representing a 43% gain from current prices. Remain bullish above $156.

Qualcomm was trading around $183 Thursday.

Looking at the weekly stock chart over the past five years, one can see how round-number theory came into play over an 18-month period. A bearish descending triangle formed between the summer of 2022 and late 2023, featuring a triple bottom just above the $100 level. The stock broke above the pattern. This is a bullish sign, as false moves are often followed quickly by moves in the opposite direction. It then more than doubled over the next eight months, until a bearish shooting star candle formed in June 2024. That started a current weekly double-bottom base with a bullish piercing line candle the week ended April 11, 2025, and the lows earlier in April. Notice the strong volume over the past two weeks, with a 9% gain last week and an additional 5% gain this week so far.

Momentum is building as Qualcomm breaks out, suggesting the path of least resistance is higher.

Doug Busch is the senior technical analyst at Barron's Investor Circle . His technical view is added to stock picks, including those published exclusively for Investor Circle readers. A glossary of technical terms is updated regularly with new entries.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
